title: Paper-and-pencil game
content:
Paper-and-pencil games or paper-and-pen games (or some variation on those terms) are game s that can be played solely with paper and pencils (or other writing implements), usually without erasing. Examples of paper-and-pencil games are Tic-tac-toe, Sprouts, and Dots and Boxes. Other examples include: Hangman, Connect 5, M.A.S.H., Battleship, Paper soccer, Golf, WedgoLogic, MLine, and Spellbinder.

Additionally, the term is used to distinguish role-playing game s of the tabletop variety from role-playing video game s, although those traditional role-playing games do not necessarily use either paper or pencils. In some board game s, particularly abstract strategy game s like Gomoku and Connect Four, a piece, once played, will not be moved or removed from the board. Such games can be played either as board games or as paper-and-pencil games, while many other paper-and-pencil games require the use of writing utensils.
